t: Which is larger: 1/2 or 1/4?
e: 1/2 is larger. When you split something into 2 pieces, each piece is bigger than when you split into 4.

t: Which is larger: 1/2 or 1/8?
e: 1/2 is larger. Splitting into 2 pieces gives much bigger pieces than splitting into 8.

t: Which is larger: 1/4 or 1/8?
e: 1/4 is larger. Fewer pieces means each piece is bigger.

t: Which is the smallest: 1/2, 1/4, or 1/8?
e: 1/8 is the smallest. When you split into 8 pieces, each piece is very small.

t: Which is the largest: 1/2, 1/4, or 1/8?
e: 1/2 is the largest. Splitting into only 2 pieces gives the biggest pieces.

t: You get 1/2 of a cookie or 1/8 of the same cookie. Which gives you more cookie?
e: 1/2 gives you more. Half a cookie is a lot more than one eighth of a cookie!

t: A pizza is cut into 4 equal slices. Another same-size pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Which pizza has BIGGER slices?
o: Both have the same size slices
e: The pizza cut into 4 slices has bigger slices. Fewer cuts = bigger pieces.

t: Which piece of pie is bigger: 1 fourth or 1 half?
e: 1 half is bigger. A half is a larger piece than a fourth.

t: Sam has 1/4 of a brownie. Ella has 1/2 of the same brownie. Who has more?
e: Ella has more. 1/2 is a bigger piece than 1/4.

t: Which is bigger: 3/4 or 1/4?
e: 3/4 is bigger. 3 fourths is more than 1 fourth -- it is 3 pieces instead of 1.

t: Which is bigger: 2/8 or 7/8?
e: 7/8 is bigger. 7 pieces out of 8 is more than 2 pieces out of 8.

t: Would you rather have 1/8 of a cake or 1/2 of the same cake?
e: 1/2 gives you much more cake. Half a cake is way bigger than one eighth!

t: 2 kids each get 1 piece of pizza. One pizza was cut into 2 slices, the other into 8 slices. Who got a bigger piece?
o: The kid with a slice from the 8-slice pizza
The kid with a slice from the 2-slice pizza
e: The kid who got a slice from the 2-slice pizza got 1/2. The other got 1/8. 1/2 > 1/8.

t: Which is bigger: 5/8 or 2/8?
e: 5/8 is bigger. When the bottom number is the same, just compare the top numbers: 5 > 2.

t: Which is bigger: 1/4 or 3/4?
e: 3/4 is bigger. 3 pieces out of 4 is more than 1 piece out of 4.

t: A ribbon is cut into 8 equal pieces. Is 1 piece bigger or smaller than half the ribbon?
e: 1 piece out of 8 is 1/8. Half the ribbon is 4/8. Since 1/8 < 4/8, one piece is smaller than half.

t: Which fraction shows MORE of a shape: 6/8 or 3/8?
e: 6/8 shows more. 6 parts shaded out of 8 is more than 3 parts shaded out of 8.

t: Mia has 1/2 of a sandwich. Jake has 1/4 of the same sandwich. Who has the smaller piece?
e: Jake has the smaller piece. 1/4 is smaller than 1/2.

t: Which is less: 1/8 or 1/4?
e: 1/8 is less. More pieces means smaller pieces, so eighths are smaller than fourths.

t: A pie is cut into 4 equal pieces. 2 pieces are cherry and 2 are apple. Which flavor takes up more of the pie?
o: They take up the same amount
e: Both flavors have 2 out of 4 pieces. 2/4 = 2/4, so they take up the same amount.
